title: Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib
tagline: Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib — heritage city of Delhi

overview.short: Gurdwara Damdama Sahib is a gurdwara (Sikh place of worship) located near Humayun's Tomb on the Outer Ring Road in New Delhi, India.
overview.description: Gurdwara Damdama Sahib is a gurdwara (Sikh place of worship) located near Humayun's Tomb on the Outer Ring Road in New Delhi, India.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Humayun's tomb
topPlaces.description: Humayun's tomb (Persian: Maqbara-i Humayun) is the tomb of the Mughal Emperor Humayun located in Delhi, India. The tomb was commissioned by Humayun's first wife and chief consort, Empress Bega Begum, under her patronage in 1558, and designed by Persian architects Mirak Mirza Ghiyas and his son, Sayyid Muhammad, whom she personally chose.

topPlaces.name: Nila Gumbad
topPlaces.description: Nila Gumbad (lit. 'Blue Dome') is a tomb located within the Humayun's Tomb complex at Delhi, India. Historians are unsure about the identity of the person who has been buried.

topPlaces.name: Tombs of Battashewala Complex
topPlaces.description: Tombs of Battashewala Complex is an Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) protected monument in Nizamuddin East, Delhi. The funerary complex, consists of three Mughal period tombs, known as the Bara Batashewala Mahal, the Chota Batashewala Mahal, an unidentified Mughal tomb and arched compound wall enclosures.

topPlaces.name: Arab Serai
topPlaces.description: Arab Serai is a 16th century caravanserai within the Humayun's tomb complex at Delhi, India. It is said to have been built by Mughal emperor Humayun's widow Haji Begum.

topPlaces.name: Nizamuddin East
topPlaces.description: Nizamuddin East is an affluent residential colony in South East Delhi, India. It is located on Mathura Road and is home to Humayun's Tomb, as well as that of Abdul Rahim Khan-I-Khana.

topPlaces.name: Sarai Kale Khan
topPlaces.description: Sarai Kale Khan is a neighborhood located in the South East Delhi district of Delhi and a major multimodal transport hub offering convenient transportation options, between Delhi Metro’s Pink line, nearby Sarai Kale Khan ISBT, semi-high-speed RRTS station, the planned high-speed rail and the adjacent Hazrat Nizamuddin Railway Station (one of Delhi's five major railway hubs).

topPlaces.name: Tomb of Isa Khan
topPlaces.description: The tomb of the noble Isa Khan Niazi is located in the Humayun's Tomb complex in Delhi, India. The mausoleum, octagonal in shape and built mainly of red sandstone, was built in 1547–1548 during the reign of Sher Shah Suri.

topPlaces.name: Indraprastha Park
topPlaces.description: The Millennium Park is a park on the Outer Ring Road in the east of Delhi, India. It was constructed in 2004 by the Delhi Development Authority.

items.note: Humayun's tomb (Persian: Maqbara-i Humayun) is the tomb of the Mughal Emperor Humayun located in Delhi, India. The tomb was commissioned by Humayun's first wife and chief consort, Empress Bega Begum, under her patronage in 1558, and designed by Persian architects Mirak Mirza Ghiyas and his son, Sayyid Muhammad, whom she personally chose.
Nila Gumbad (lit. 'Blue Dome') is a tomb located within the Humayun's Tomb complex at Delhi, India. Historians are unsure about the identity of the person who has been buried.

Tombs of Battashewala Complex is an Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) protected monument in Nizamuddin East, Delhi. The funerary complex, consists of three Mughal period tombs, known as the Bara Batashewala Mahal, the Chota Batashewala Mahal, an unidentified Mughal tomb and arched compound wall enclosures.
Arab Serai is a 16th century caravanserai within the Humayun's tomb complex at Delhi, India. It is said to have been built by Mughal emperor Humayun's widow Haji Begum.
Nizamuddin East is an affluent residential colony in South East Delhi, India. It is located on Mathura Road and is home to Humayun's Tomb, as well as that of Abdul Rahim Khan-I-Khana.

Sarai Kale Khan is a neighborhood located in the South East Delhi district of Delhi and a major multimodal transport hub offering convenient transportation options, between Delhi Metro’s Pink line, nearby Sarai Kale Khan ISBT, semi-high-speed RRTS station, the planned high-speed rail and the adjacent Hazrat Nizamuddin Railway Station (one of Delhi's five major railway hubs).

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Tomb of Isa Khan & around

items.note: The tomb of the noble Isa Khan Niazi is located in the Humayun's Tomb complex in Delhi, India. The mausoleum, octagonal in shape and built mainly of red sandstone, was built in 1547–1548 during the reign of Sher Shah Suri.
The Millennium Park is a park on the Outer Ring Road in the east of Delhi, India. It was constructed in 2004 by the Delhi Development Authority.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (16 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (6 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurdwara Dam Dama Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Humayun's tomb, Nila Gumbad, Tombs of Battashewala Complex, Arab Serai, Nizamuddin East.
